text: When designing a REST API, following best practices ensures it is easy to use and maintain. Start by naming your endpoints clearly with nouns, like `/users` or `/orders`, and use HTTP methods (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE) to perform actions. Status codes are important for clear communication: use 200 for success, 201 for created resources, 404 for not found, and 500 for server errors. Good documentation helps developers understand how to use your API without confusion. Finally, always include versioning, for example `/v1/users`, so that changes don't break existing users. These practices create reliable, user-friendly APIs.
